items.markdown:
![Duck Hunting Archives • Arkansas Game & Fish Commission](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/04282011-1118-0139-0000-f662d2094358:4)
For the 2025-2026 season, Arkansas duck hunting generally runs from `late November through January` , split into three segments: Nov. 22–Dec. 1, 2025, Dec. 10–23, 2025, and Dec. 27, 2025–Jan. 31, 2026. A special early teal season is also held Sept. 20–28, 2025. The daily bag limit is 6 ducks, including a maximum of 4 mallards.[[1]](https://www.agfc.com/hunting/waterfowl/waterfowl-dates-rules-regulations/#:~:text=Here%20are%20some%20other%20special%20duck%20season,Same%20as%20regular%20duck%20and%20goose%20seasons)[[2]](https://www.whisperingoakshunting.com/duck-hunting/#:~:text=Whispering%20Oaks%20Hunting%20Lodge%20is%20a%204%2C000-acre,*%20Call%20%28870%29%20243-5877%20*%20Email%20info@whisperingoakshunting.com)

**Key 2025-2026 Arkansas Duck Season Details** 

- **Regular Duck Season:** Nov. 22–Dec. 1, 2025; Dec. 10–23, 2025; and Dec. 27, 2025–Jan. 31, 2026.
- **Special Early Teal Season:** Sept. 20–28, 2025.
- **Special Youth/Military/Veteran Hunt:** Feb. 7–8, 2026.
- **Daily Bag Limit:** 6 ducks (may include up to 4 mallards, 3 wood ducks, 2 redheads, etc.).
- **Required Licenses:** Arkansas resident/nonresident license, [Arkansas Waterfowl Stamp](https://www.agfc.com/hunting/waterfowl/waterfowl-dates-rules-regulations/), Federal Duck Stamp , and HIP registration.[[1]](https://www.agfc.com/hunting/waterfowl/waterfowl-dates-rules-regulations/#:~:text=Here%20are%20some%20other%20special%20duck%20season,Same%20as%20regular%20duck%20and%20goose%20seasons)[[2]](https://www.whisperingoakshunting.com/duck-hunting/#:~:text=Whispering%20Oaks%20Hunting%20Lodge%20is%20a%204%2C000-acre,*%20Call%20%28870%29%20243-5877%20*%20Email%20info@whisperingoakshunting.com)[[3]](https://www.cuppedwingsguideservice.com/season-info#:~:text=The%20Arkansas%20main%20waterfowl%20season%20is%20September,Stamp%20%28DSR%29**%20*%20**Federal%20Duck%20Stamp%20%28FDE%29**)

It is highly recommended to check the official Arkansas Game & Fish Commission website for any last-minute, site-specific regulation changes or WMA (Wildlife Management Area) restrictions.

expanded_element.title: Waterfowl Hunter Numbers Drop
expanded_element.description: The most popular duck in North America—the mallard—tops all other species in harvest. U.S. hunters shot 3.47 million last season. Arkansas led with 530,183 mallards, but the next states on the list might surprise people. Oklahoma was second with 201,683, while Missouri ranked third with 187,251.
